response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array however for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference  

for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference  

***  

the solution involves calculating the absolute differences for all elements except those at indices that are multiples of 3 and then calculating the square of the differences for those elements. since the array is sorted, we can use prefix sums to efficiently compute the absolute differences.  

for the absolute differences, the formula is sum(nums[i] - nums[j]) for j < i and sum(nums[j] - nums[i]) for j > i. since the array is sorted, this can be simplified to nums[i] * i - prefix_sum[i] + (n - i - 1) * nums[i] - (prefix_sum[n] - prefix_sum[i]).  

for the square differences, the formula is sum((nums[i] - nums[j])^2) for j < i and sum((nums[j] - nums[i])^2) for j > i. since the array is sorted, this can be simplified to sum((nums[i] - nums[j])^2) for j < i and sum((nums[j] - nums[i])^2) for j > i.  

the code will iterate through each element of the array, check if the index is a multiple of 3, and calculate the appropriate sum. the final result array will contain the computed values for each index.
